name: Toffee Butter Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, warm chopped chocolate, 2 tablespoons butter, and 1/3 cup whipping cream over low heat. Stir occasionally until the chocolate melts and the mixture is smooth. Allow to cool.
2. Generously grease a 9-inch springform pan.
3. In a medium bowl, combine grated chocolate and half a cup of finely crushed candy. Sprinkle one-third of this mixture over the bottom and halfway up the sides of the pan. Set aside.
4. In a large mixer bowl, beat together 1 1/2 cups butter, brown sugar, and vanilla until light and fluffy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating for three minutes after each addition (this is very important).
5. Fold in whipped cream and another cup of coarsely crushed candy. Pour half of the cream mixture into the pan.
6. Drizzle the melted and cooled chocolate mixture over the surface to within 1/2 inch of the outside edge. Smooth the chocolate layer.
7. Gently spoon the remaining cream mixture over the chocolate; smooth the top.
8. Sprinkle with the remaining chocolate candy mixture. Using the back of a spoon, lightly press into the surface of the torte.
9. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. The torte may be tightly covered and frozen for up to 1 month. To loosen the torte, run a thin knife around the inside edge of the pan; remove the side.
